design_philosophy.core_loop: Select character pair → traverse level clearing enemy nodes → collect gold/power-ups → manage resources between levels → defeat boss → progress to next world
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing character selection (speed vs. power) against resource constraints; cooperative partner AI reliability vs. solo vulnerability
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Mercenary infiltrators battling steampunk automatons and monsters across a corrupted Victorian England to stop a sentient reality-warping machine
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Character hiring system creates permanent loadout commitment with no mid-run swapping; cooperative AI is mandatory even in single-player, forcing player to rely on partner's decision-making; character archetypes have radical stat tradeoffs (heavy slow damage-dealers vs. fast fragile spreaders), demanding playstyle specialization

difficulty.checkpointing: Per-level checkpoint system; mid-level node activation gates progression
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: character selection fundamentally alters difficulty curve (Navvie/Thug vs. Gentleman/Preacher create different challenge profiles); resource management between levels provides strategic difficulty scaling through upgrades

progression.upgrade_system: Mid-level shop system: collect gold during play to purchase weapon upgrades, health increases, item unlocks, and character attribute improvements at designated save points after every second level
progression.unlock_mechanism: Keys found in levels unlock doors/puzzle gates; weapon nodes must be activated by weapon fire to open final doors; character progression unlocked through earned currency
replayability_hooks: Six character roster with distinct stat/weapon profiles encourages different character pair combinations
Character hiring creates strategic team-building puzzle; cost constraints force meaningful choices
Resource scarcity creates high-risk/high-reward decisions (save gold vs. upgrade now)
Partner AI behavior varies by character type, altering cooperative dynamics

world_building_techniques: Introductory text/narration establishing steampunk setting and Chaos Engine threat
Character roster embodies thematic diversity (Navvie, Gentleman, Preacher, Mercenary, Brigand, Thug)
Environmental progression through four distinct worlds showing Engine's corruption
Final reveal: narrator revealed as Baron Fortesque trapped within the Chaos Engine itself

boss_design.philosophy: Single final boss encounter representing the Chaos Engine as the narrative climax; boss is sentient corrupting force rather than traditional combat opponent
notable_bosses: Chaos Engine (final boss): sentient reality-warping machine with Baron Fortesque assimilated within; described as containing/transforming matter and space-time itself

faction_indicators: Mercenary profession of player characters establishes mercenary faction
Baron Fortesque and Royal Society represent corrupted/failed institutional authority
Chaos Engine as rogue AI threat suggests technology-vs-tradition conflict
danger_signals: Quarantine of British Isles; all ships attacked on approach
Royal Family and Parliament fled; British Empire 'in tatters'
Vile monsters and automata proliferating across countryside
Prehistoric beasts resurrected, indicating reality destabilization
environmental_storytelling: Four progressive worlds showing escalating corruption from Engine
Steampunk aesthetic juxtaposing Victorian era with retro-engineered future technology
Mazes and puzzles suggest abandoned infrastructure repurposed by hostile forces
Character archetypes (blue-collar Navvie, aristocratic Gentleman, clergy Preacher) reflect stratified society caught in crisis
